nodes.name: VU
nodes.summary: VU表示 IUCN 易危等级，当前关联694种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、索马里鸵鸟、大斑几维鸟等694种鸟类。

nodes.name: 园林莺
nodes.summary: 园林莺（Garden warbler，学名 Sylvia borin），在图谱中标记为易危（VU）物种，已关联6个分布地点节点，主要威胁包括小种群风险、气候变化。
nodes.shortSummary: 庭园林莺（學名：Sylvia borin，英語：Garden Warbler，台湾作園林鶯），是雀形目鶲科鶯屬下的一種鳥類。這個物種為植食性，廣泛棲息於擁有豐富的底層植被的灌木地、林地中。在夏季時，這個物種於歐洲繁殖，到了冬季時則遷徙至撒哈拉沙漠以南過冬。園林鶯以其歌聲聞名，是…

nodes.name: 小种群风险
nodes.summary: 种群规模过小导致遗传多样性下降和脆弱性上升。

nodes.name: 气候变化
nodes.summary: 长期改变水文节律与生态系统稳定性的全球变化因素。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: woodwardi (Sharpe, 1877) }} The garden warbler (Sylvia borin) is a common and widespread small bird that breeds in most of Europe and in the Palearctic to western Siberia

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The garden warbler is strongly migratory, wintering in sub-Saharan Africa

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: Deciduous woodland is the preferred breeding habitat.|thumb|alt=A woodland glade The garden warbler breeds in most of Europe between the isotherms and east across temperate Asia to the Yenisei River in Siberia

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: All populations are migratory, wintering in sub-Saharan Africa as far south as South Africa. Birds from central Europe initially migrate to the southwest, reorientating to the south or southeast once in Africa, although Scandinavian migrants may head south through the Alps and across the Mediterranean Sea

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: All populations are migratory, wintering in sub-Saharan Africa as far south as South Africa. Birds from central Europe initially migrate to the southwest, reorientating to the south or southeast once in Africa, although Scandinavian migrants may head south through the Alps and across the Mediterranean Sea

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: woodwardi winters in eastern and southern Africa. Movements in Africa are poorly known, although at least some birds return to the same location in subsequent years. There are only a handful of records of birds recorded in Europe in winter, from Corsica, the UK and Ireland

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Despite a small population decline in much of its European range, the bird's breeding distribution is expanding northwards in Scandinavia

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: There is no evidence of any serious decline in numbers, so it is classified by the International Union for Conservation of Nature as being of least concern. There has been a slight decline in numbers in Europe since 1980, although the Scandinavian population is growing. Climate change appears to be affecting the migration pattern of the garden warbler and blackcap
